<!--
Template for docs/systems/<name>.md.
Copy this file, fill in each section, and delete this comment block.
See docs/STYLE.md for writing and linking conventions.
-->

# <System Name>

## Purpose

<!-- What this system does and why it exists. -->

## Questions this doc answers

<!-- List the practical questions a reader should be able to answer after reading this doc. -->

## Scope

<!-- What this system includes. -->

## Non-scope

<!-- What this system does NOT own, especially where the boundary is easy to misunderstand. -->

## Key concepts

<!-- Domain concepts, states, entities, and terms used by this system. Use Title Case for terms
     defined in docs/glossary.md when it improves clarity. -->

## How the system works

<!-- Behavioral-level explanation: mechanics, responsibilities, interactions. Not a line-by-line
     restatement of the implementation. -->

## Important flows

<!-- Major flows this system participates in. Link to docs/flows/ for anything cross-system,
     multi-step, or important enough to review independently. -->

## Data and state

<!-- Persisted data, in-memory state, caches, or derived state this system owns. -->

## Interfaces and entry points

<!-- Main ways this system is used: CLI commands, public functions, event handlers, etc. -->

## Dependencies

<!-- Other systems, crates, or infrastructure this system depends on. -->

## Downstream effects

<!-- What else may be affected when this system changes. -->

## Invariants and assumptions

<!-- Rules that must remain true for correct behavior. -->

## Error handling

<!-- Important failure modes and how they're handled. -->

## Security and privacy notes

<!-- Security-sensitive behavior, permissions, or data handling. Omit only if clearly irrelevant. -->

## Observability and debugging

<!-- How to debug this system: logs, commands, common symptoms. -->

## Testing notes

<!-- Important tests and how to validate changes. -->

## Common pitfalls

<!-- Things contributors or agents are likely to misunderstand. -->

## Source map

<!-- Links to the most important source files. Don't list every file unless the system is small. -->

## Related docs

<!-- Links to related system/flow docs, ADRs, glossary entries. -->
